WTI.data_anomaly: fast_info prev_close $94.49 (-1.7%) vs history prev_close $87.25 (+6.5%). Using fast_info as headline; history used for technicals only.

macro_summary: Markets remain in fragile equilibrium as the US-Iran conflict enters its second week. SPY fell 0.24% and QQQ 0.13% on March 11, as the March 9 relief bounce continues to lose steam — per the news digest, the 100-day moving average break remains unresolved and breadth confirms a tactical rally rather than structural recovery. Per CNBC, investors are bracing for what comes next as the conflict stretches into its second week, with the 6,500 SPY support level in focus if weakness persists. The CPI report released today (March 12) is the single most important near-term catalyst for rate expectations, with the 10-year Treasury yield already rising to 4.21% and the dollar (DXY) firm at 99.47 on a hawkish pause narrative.
commodities_note: WTI crude shows a data anomaly in today's pipeline: one source implies a large daily drop to $92.90 (-1.68%) while the five-day trend is only modestly negative — the more reliable read is that oil has stabilised near $93 after a sharp ~30% decline from the $120+ highs seen when the Hormuz crisis first broke, per AP News and CNBC coverage. The demand destruction narrative is now the primary driver over geopolitical risk premium. Gold at $5,161.50 is essentially flat (+0.06%) and remains at historically elevated levels. GLD is already extremely extended — per today's commodity signal follow-through rule, there is no attractive entry for a gold vehicle at current levels. The dollar's firmness (DXY 99.47) is a headwind for commodity prices and tech multiples simultaneously.

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. DBS is trading at SGD 55.19, sitting 4.2% below its declining 50-day average of SGD 57.60, with RSI at 38 in neutral-bearish territory — the technicals reflect two weeks of sustained selling pressure. The core drag is structural: per the macro narrative context, all three Singapore banks reported declining net interest income in Q4 2025 earnings (SGX filings), and the rate pause narrative (US 10-year at 4.21%) removes the catalyst that had previously supported bank margins. At 2.27 times book and a 14.4 times trailing price-to-earnings ratio, valuation is not compelling enough to justify buying into a declining trend — the 5.49% dividend yield (data shows 549 basis points) is attractive on paper but doesn't offset the margin compression risk. The 200-day average at SGD 51.72 is the next meaningful structural support, roughly 6% below current price. Not attractive at current levels — revisit only if the 50-day average stabilises and turns upward, or if Singapore's Monetary Authority signals a policy pivot that supports bank margins.

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — OCBC has slipped 0.82% below its 50-day average of SGD 20.85, removing it from the entry zone that justified the WATCH signal. OCBC was the only Singapore bank with year-over-year profit growth in Q4 2025 (per the macro narrative context), and its rising 50-day average distinguishes it from DBS and UOB — so the fundamental thesis is comparatively stronger. However, with price now below the 50-day average, RSI at 43.9, and low volume signal, the technicals don't support a fresh entry. Support is distant at SGD 16.62 and SGD 15.68 — the 200-day average at SGD 18.02 sits roughly 13% below current price and is the level to watch if the STI continues weakening below 5,000. Revisit WATCH if OCBC reclaims SGD 20.85 with improving volume, which would confirm the relative strength thesis is holding.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. UOB is 2.82% below its 50-day average of SGD 37.22, RSI at 41, and down 6.54% over the past month — the weakest one-month performance in the Singapore cluster. The forward price-to-earnings ratio of 9.9 times is the most attractive valuation in the cluster, and the rising 50-day average is a mildly positive structural note, but neither is sufficient to offset the sector headwinds from net interest margin compression and the rate pause narrative. Current price at SGD 36.17 coincides exactly with the lower resistance zone noted in the data — a failure to hold here could open a move toward SGD 34.23 support. Not attractive until the 50-day average reclaims SGD 37.22 with volume confirmation or rate-cut expectations shift materially.

C6L_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Singapore Airlines is the cleanest setup in the Singapore cluster right now — price at SGD 6.67 is just 0.76% above its rising 50-day average of SGD 6.62, RSI at 46.7 in neutral territory, and the stock has only been above the 50-day average for three sessions, suggesting this is an early-stage recovery attempt rather than an extended trend. The airline is caught between two opposing forces: the Iran conflict is disrupting Gulf airspace, with SIA and Scoot reportedly cancelling Middle East flights per the Singapore Business Review, which creates a near-term revenue headwind; but a sustained oil price decline toward $80 would meaningfully reduce fuel costs, which are SIA's largest operating expense. At 17.5 times forward earnings and an enterprise value-to-EBITDA of 7 times, valuation is reasonable for the sector. First resistance at SGD 6.78, then SGD 7.20. Entry becomes attractive if price pulls back to the SGD 6.50-6.62 zone near the 50-day average on any broad market dip — current level is right at the edge of acceptable entry range but volume is low, which argues for patience.

Z74_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Singtel is 4.86% above its rising 50-day average of SGD 4.74, RSI at 54, and has been building momentum over the past month (+3.97%). At 28 sessions above the 50-day average, it's two sessions short of the 30-session threshold required for a trend exception — so entry here is technically not supported yet, though the setup is maturing. The structural thesis rests on the Asia United Gateway East consortium (per LinkedIn/Singtel post) and ongoing 5G/digital infrastructure expansion across Southeast Asia, which provides multi-year revenue visibility. The forward price-to-earnings ratio of 24.2 times represents a premium to the Singapore bank cluster, justified by higher growth expectations, but the dividend yield of 2.63% is notably lower than peers. Resistance at SGD 5.15 is the near-term ceiling. Becomes actionable if it pulls back to SGD 4.80-4.90 near the 50-day average, or if it achieves 30+ sessions above the average with RSI remaining below 60.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. NVDA is sitting almost exactly on its 50-day average of $185.70 (current price $186.03, just 0.18% above), with RSI at 51 — technically neutral in every dimension. The Blackwell Ultra ramp thesis is intact per NVDA's Q4 FY2026 earnings call, with Q1 FY2027 guidance of $78 billion implying the revenue ramp is pulling forward faster than analysts had modelled. No entry dislocation exists right now — price is fair relative to the 50-day average, but RSI and volume give no positive trigger. Support at $177.41 is where the setup improves. Upgrades to Watch on a pullback to $177-180 or on a volume breakout above $189.

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. AMD is 5.57% below its declining 50-day average of $216.92, RSI at 48.4, and has shed 5.17% over the past month — the declining 50-day average is the key warning here, as it signals the trend has not merely paused but reversed. The reported Meta GPU supply deal (reportedly MI450-based, though unverified per catalyst notes — needs SEC filing confirmation) would be transformative if confirmed, validating AMD as a credible alternative to NVDA at hyperscale; but unverified catalysts don't justify entering a deteriorating trend. The trailing price-to-earnings ratio of 78.8 times and enterprise value-to-EBITDA of 48.5 times leave minimal margin for error if earnings disappoint. Support is distant at $158 and $149 — the 200-day average at $190 is the nearest structural floor, roughly 7% below current price. Not attractive until AMD reclaims the $216 50-day average with improving volume, or the Meta deal is confirmed via SEC filing.

INTC.entry_block: BLOCKED: +5.1% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=3 (<30), RSI 56 (>=55).
INTC.signal_rationale: Signal adjusted from the raw BUY (implied by recent upgrade in the news digest) to WATCH — Intel is 5.07% above its 50-day average of $45.66 with RSI at 55.9, both exceeding the thresholds required for a trend exception entry, and the entry block is in force. The Intel thesis is compelling: the CHIPS Act award of up to $7.86 billion in direct funding (per the US Department of Commerce, cited via GPEC) underpins a $100 billion domestic manufacturing expansion, and the AI datacenter power demand cycle is a structural driver for US-centric foundry capacity. The High-NA EUV ramp with ASML (Intel 14A node, per ASML Q4 2025 earnings call) represents a technology leadership inflection point. Intel has only been above the 50-day average for three sessions and RSI is at the boundary — the setup needs to either pull back to the $44-46 zone near the 50-day average, or hold above it for 30+ sessions with RSI cooling below 55, before a clean entry materialises. First resistance at $51.49, then $54.60.

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. ASML at $1,386.68 is 2.14% above its rising 50-day average of $1,357.58 — within the slightly extended zone, which is acceptable with a note. RSI at 49.4 is neutral-bearish and provides no red flag. The fundamental case is straightforward: as the sole manufacturer of the extreme ultraviolet lithography machines required to make the world's most advanced chips, ASML reported record Q4 orders of €13.2 billion driven by AI demand (per LinkedIn/Bloomberg coverage), and is awaiting results of a US government Section 232 investigation into semiconductor equipment — an overhang worth monitoring. Forward price-to-earnings of 31.8 times is premium but defensible given the monopoly position and multi-year order backlog. China export control risk is the primary overhang — Trump's 100% tariffs and software bans (per the macro narrative) add uncertainty to ASML's China revenue. Entry slightly above the 50-day average is acceptable here; the $1,350-1,365 zone is the ideal entry range. First resistance at $1,491.

MU.entry_block: BLOCKED: +9.3% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=3 (<30), RSI 56 (>=55).
MU.signal_rationale: Signal adjusted from BUY to WATCH — Micron is 9.32% above its 50-day average of $382.98 with RSI at 56, both exceeding the thresholds for a clean entry, and the pipeline entry block is in force. The memory thesis is genuine: Micron's high-bandwidth memory capacity expansion (per MU Q1 FY2026 earnings call) directly serves NVDA's Blackwell and Vera Rubin requirements, and the company is among the highest capex growers in semis. Note that Micron's CEO projects memory prices rising approximately 40% through Q2 2026 — but this is a bullish call from a seller, and independent corroboration is needed before treating it as a market consensus view. Forward price-to-earnings of 8.9 times is genuinely attractive for a memory cycle play if the AI demand thesis holds. The entry zone opens below $400, ideally on a pullback toward the $363-382 range where the 50-day average and prior support converge. First resistance at $437-455.

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Amazon is 5.17% below its declining 50-day average of $224.25, and both the 50-day and 200-day averages ($224.64) are declining — a rare double-declining structure that signals sustained institutional selling. The $200 billion AI capital expenditure announcement (per AMZN Q4 2025 earnings call and 10-K) sent shares down 10% on missed Q1 operating income guidance, and the key unresolved question is when that spending converts to earnings. Forward price-to-earnings of 22.8 times and enterprise value-to-EBITDA of 16 times are not stretched, but they don't compensate for declining trend momentum. Support at $211.22 is being tested — a break below it opens a move toward $196.93. The 200-day average at $224.64 is now overhead resistance, not support. Not attractive until AMZN reclaims and holds above the $224 50-day average.

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Alphabet is 3.46% below its declining 50-day average of $319.48, RSI at 47, and has shed 4.86% over the past month amid the broader mega-cap concentration unwind. The fundamental story is not broken — Alphabet's first-ever $100 billion quarterly revenue (per NDTV/Google Q4 earnings) was powered by AI-driven search and cloud growth, and 7th-generation TPUs are reportedly being used by Meta, Anthropic, and Apple (per Google Cloud Next 2025 keynote, though the Meta chip cancellation claim is unverified). But a declining 50-day average in a rising-rate environment (10-year at 4.21%) suppresses the premium that growth investors are willing to pay. Forward price-to-earnings of 23 times is reasonable on fundamentals but the trend needs to stabilise first. Not attractive until price reclaims $319 with volume; the $308 area is the near-term line in the sand, and a break below $308 would expose the $237 support level.

LITE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +35.1% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.
LITE.sma50_warning: SMA50 is 26% below price — pullback to SMA50 would require a crash-level move. Use shorter-term support levels for entry guidance instead.
LITE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Lumentum at $672 is 35.1% above its 50-day average — an extreme extension that triggers a hard entry block with no exceptions. The structural catalysts are real: Lumentum was confirmed added to the S&P 500 on March 9 (per S&P Dow Jones Indices press release), which drives forced passive buying, and the company reported record Q2 FY2026 revenue of $665.5 million, up 65.5% year-over-year (per catalyst notes). The reported $2 billion strategic investment from NVDA into Lumentum for AI optical interconnect components is unverified — it needs an SEC 8-K filing confirmation before being treated as fact. Per the sma50_warning, a pullback to the 50-day average would require a crash-level decline of ~26%; shorter-term support at $528 is the relevant entry reference. Not actionable until price pulls back to at least $620-640 on the next broad market correction, preferably with RSI cooling below 50.

COHR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Coherent dropped 5.47% today and is down 8.53% over five days — profit-taking is arriving exactly as the photonics extension thesis warned. At $251.41, the stock remains 14.06% above its 50-day average of $220.42, which is above the 5% hard block threshold but below the 20% extreme extension level, and RSI at 52.8 is below 55. However, with only 129 sessions above the 50-day average and a rising 50-day average, the Extended Ticker Decision Tree requires checking the driver: the S&P 500 inclusion (confirmed per S&P Dow Jones Indices) is a structural event, and the reported $2 billion NVDA investment (unverified — needs 8-K) would add further structural weight if confirmed. The pullback toward the $204.57 support level is where the setup genuinely improves — that's roughly 19% below current price, closer to the 50-day average. Watch for entry in the $210-225 zone if profit-taking continues; first resistance if it stabilises is $300.

XLE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +10.9% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 67 (>=55).
XLE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. The energy sector ETF is 10.88% above its 50-day average of $51.39 with RSI at 67.3 — both exceed the thresholds for a trend exception, and the entry block is hard. The oil thesis remains structurally interesting: the Strait of Hormuz carries roughly 20% of the world's oil supply (per Rystad Energy as cited in CBS News), and Kuwait has announced precautionary production cuts (per CNBC) while Iraqi output has effectively collapsed. But WTI has already fallen ~30% from its $120+ highs to ~$93 on demand destruction fears, meaning the geopolitical risk premium that drove XLE up 14% in Q1 is unwinding faster than the supply disruption thesis can sustain. XLE has been above its 50-day average for 50 sessions, which meets the duration requirement, and the 50-day average is rising — but RSI at 67.3 remains the blocking factor. Becomes actionable if XLE pulls back to the $54-56 range and RSI cools to below 60, which would approximate $52-53 equivalent on further oil price stabilisation.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 82x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Palantir at $151.60 sits 2.23% below its declining 50-day average of $155.06 — technically in no-man's land, neither at a compelling entry level nor at a support breakdown. The defense AI thesis is the strongest fundamental story on the watchlist right now: per Army contract databases and a DoD press release, a $10 billion Army IDIQ framework ceiling (note: this is a ceiling amount, not firm revenue) and a $448 million Navy ShipOS deal have driven a 15% weekly gain. Rosenblatt raised its price target to $200 and Daiwa upgraded to Buy with a $180 target (per catalyst notes). The Pentagon AI ban (reportedly affecting Maven Smart Systems' use of Claude, per Yahoo Finance) is a short-term headwind, but per the catalyst notes, Palantir's 2026 revenue guidance of $7.18-7.20 billion implies 61% year-over-year growth. The valuation is the hard stop: a forward price-to-earnings ratio of 82 times and enterprise value-to-EBITDA of 247 times price in a vast amount of that growth already. The soft entry block requires an explicit earnings growth catalyst with timeline to upgrade — the IDIQ framework is a ceiling, not a firm revenue trigger. No action until either a pullback to $140-145 or the IDIQ converts to firm contract awards visible in quarterly revenue.

VUAA_L.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. The Vanguard S&P 500 UCITS ETF at $130.28 is 1.70% below its declining 50-day average of $132.53, with RSI at 44 and volume low — a straightforward reflection of the unresolved S&P 500 technical breakdown. The index has failed to reclaim the 100-day moving average after the March 9 bounce, and the 6,500 SPY support level remains the key downside reference. The 200-day average at $126.61 is approximately 2.8% below current price — meaningful as a structural floor but not distant enough to feel like a safety net in a geopolitical risk environment. Not attractive for new broad index exposure until SPY reclaims and holds the 100-day moving average with multi-session follow-through, or a confirmed positive catalyst (CPI print today, FOMC on March 19) shifts the rate narrative toward cuts.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster is the weakest on the watchlist, with four of five names on CAUTION or WATCH and the STI below 5,000. The one divergence worth noting: OCBC (O39) was just downgraded from WATCH to CAUTION as it slipped below its 50-day average, erasing the relative strength advantage it held versus DBS and UOB — the cluster is now uniformly weak with no immediate entry candidates except SIA (C6L), which sits at its 50-day average.
singapore.thesis_status: Deteriorating — no clear entry across banking names
singapore.key_development: OCBC downgrade from WATCH to CAUTION removes the only near-entry candidate in the banking sub-cluster; SIA remains the sole watchable setup but faces Gulf airspace disruption headwinds per Singapore Business Review.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster maintains the sharpest internal divergence on the watchlist: INTC and MU carry BUY-equivalent thesis strength but are blocked from formal BUY signals by technical extension (5%+ above 50-day averages, RSI near threshold), while NVDA holds steady on its 50-day average and AMD remains in a declining trend. ASML offers the cleanest entry geometry at 2.1% above its 50-day average, making it the cluster's best-positioned name for a near-term entry if support holds. The US-centric supply chain rebalancing thesis (driven by 100% China tariffs) continues to separate domestic foundry/memory names from ASML's China-exposure overhang.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Bullish with technical entry constraints on key names
semiconductors.key_development: INTC and MU entry blocks remain in force despite BUY-equivalent fundamentals — the cluster's actionability depends on either a broad market pullback bringing prices back toward 50-day averages or multi-week trend consolidation reducing RSI.

big_tech.summary: BigTech remains the weakest cluster on fundamental momentum, with AMZN and GOOG both in declining 50-day average trends and carrying CAUTION signals. PLTR is the outlier — defense AI contract wins distinguish it from consumer/cloud mega-caps — but its extreme valuation (82 times forward earnings) keeps it at HOLD rather than Watch. The mega-cap concentration unwind narrative is intact for a third consecutive session.
big_tech.thesis_status: Deteriorating for AMZN/GOOG; PLTR stable but overvalued
big_tech.key_development: No fresh catalyst changes cluster stance today; the Pentagon AI ban affecting Maven Smart Systems (per Yahoo Finance) is a short-term headwind for PLTR that adds to the complexity of its already stretched valuation.

photonics.summary: COHR's 5.47% single-session decline confirms the profit-taking risk flagged since the March 9 S&P 500 inclusion spike — the cluster is entering a natural consolidation phase from extreme extension. Both names remain on WATCH as the structural S&P inclusion catalyst and AI optical interconnect demand thesis are intact, but the entry window only opens on continued pullback: COHR toward $210-225, LITE toward $620-640.
photonics.thesis_status: Structural thesis intact; profit-taking phase underway
photonics.key_development: COHR's -5.47% today (following -8.53% over five days) is the first meaningful technical reset since the inclusion spike — watch whether it finds support at $204-210 or accelerates toward the 50-day average at $220.

energy.summary: XLE remains technically blocked despite a compelling geopolitical supply thesis — RSI at 67.3 and 10.88% extension above the 50-day average make this a 'right story, wrong price' situation. The demand destruction narrative (WTI down ~30% from highs) is actively compressing the geopolitical risk premium that drove XLE's Q1 outperformance, and the entry block will remain in force until RSI cools below 60.
energy.thesis_status: Right thesis, technically extended — wait for reset
energy.key_development: WTI stabilising near $93 after the demand destruction selloff is a necessary condition for XLE re-entry, but RSI cooling is the binding constraint — not price level alone.

index.summary: VUAA_L mirrors the unresolved S&P 500 technical breakdown — below its declining 50-day average, VIX at 24.23 still reflecting geopolitical tail risk, and the CPI print today is the most immediate catalyst that could shift the rate narrative. No broad index entry until the 100-day moving average is reclaimed with follow-through.
index.key_development: Today's February CPI release is the pivot point: a softer-than-expected print could shift rate-cut expectations and provide the catalyst for a genuine (not tactical) index recovery attempt.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company
interconnected.reason: INTC's Intel Foundry Services and ASML's High-NA EUV customer ramp both ultimately depend on TSMC setting the pace for leading-edge chip production — Blackwell Ultra and Vera Rubin GPU volumes run through advanced nodes where TSMC is the dominant fab.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM pulls back 8-10% from current levels, aligning with a broader semi correction scenario where SPY tests the 6,500 support level.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: The dominant supplier of high-bandwidth memory for NVDA's Blackwell GPUs — MU's 40% memory price forecast (with noted seller bias) would only materialise if SK Hynix and Micron together face tighter supply; SK Hynix's order book is the independent check on MU's bullish call.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor after MU's next earnings call for independent memory pricing confirmation; add to watchlist if the KRW-denominated price corrects 10%+ on any broad emerging-market risk-off move triggered by the Hormuz conflict.

interconnected.name: Broadcom
interconnected.reason: LITE and COHR supply the laser and optical components that Broadcom integrates into its networking chips used in AI datacenter switch fabrics — the photonics cluster's S&P 500 inclusion spike signals hyperscaler optical interconnect spending is accelerating, which flows directly into Broadcom's custom AI accelerator and networking revenue.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if Broadcom pulls back 8-12% from current levels on any broad tech selloff, bringing it closer to a level consistent with its historical 50-day moving average relationship — revisit after the next hyperscaler capital expenditure update (Meta, Google Q1 earnings) for optical demand confirmation.

interconnected.name: ExxonMobil
interconnected.reason: The most direct single-stock expression of the Hormuz oil supply thesis that XLE captures as an ETF — XOM was up 4.2% in Q1 per the catalyst notes, and as the largest US energy company it has the most direct earnings sensitivity to a sustained WTI recovery above $100.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor for entry if WTI stabilises above $95 for three or more consecutive sessions and XOM pulls back 5% from current levels, reducing the RSI overhang that keeps the whole energy sector technically extended.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Designs the custom AI networking chips (electro-optical interconnects) used by hyperscalers like Amazon and Google, making it a direct beneficiary of the same optical interconnect capacity buildout that is driving LITE and COHR's revenue acceleration.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Marvell corrects 10-15% from current levels on any broad semi pullback — the cleaner entry window for the optical networking theme may emerge here before LITE and COHR complete their post-inclusion consolidation.

active_risks: Iran-US conflict halting Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic — 20% of world oil supply at risk
US-China 100% tariff escalation and semiconductor export bans reshaping global supply chains
Federal Reserve rate pause risk if February CPI (released today) surprises to the upside
geopolitical.macro_outlook: The Hormuz disruption is now in its second week and has already achieved a paradoxical outcome: WTI has fallen ~30% from its $120+ peak as demand destruction fears overwhelm the supply risk premium, per AP News and CNBC. The supply chain secondary effects — air cargo reroutes, tanker surcharges, port disruptions at Jebel Ali (per Reuters/Automotive Logistics) — are now the more insidious channel, as they embed cost inflation into corporate margins without the headline visibility of oil prices. The rate environment adds a compounding headwind: with the 10-year Treasury at 4.21% and DXY at 99.47, the dollar strength and rate pause narrative are suppressing the tech multiple expansion that would normally accompany easing geopolitical risk.

base_case.description: Hormuz conflict persists 2-4 more weeks with partial tanker traffic resuming under US naval escort; WTI stabilises in the $88-100 range; February CPI comes in at or slightly above consensus, reinforcing a Fed pause through Q2 2026; S&P 500 tests 6,500 support but holds.
base_case.market_impact: SPY range-bound between 6,500-6,800; semis (INTC, MU) outperform on US supply chain thesis while mega-cap tech (AMZN, GOOG) remain under pressure; XLE consolidates near current levels; Singapore banks flat to slightly down on rate pause confirmation.
base_case.portfolio_action: Hold current watchlist stance — no new broad index exposure. ASML near its 50-day average is the best near-term entry candidate if it holds $1,350.

optimistic_case.description: US-brokered ceasefire or Hormuz corridor agreement within 1-2 weeks; tanker traffic resumes; WTI falls toward $80-85 on demand destruction + resumed supply; CPI softer than expected, reviving rate-cut expectations for May-June 2026.
optimistic_case.market_impact: SPY relief rally back toward 6,800-7,000 (100-day moving average reclaim); tech multiples re-expand as rates expectations shift dovish; XLE sells off sharply as oil risk premium collapses; Singapore banks benefit from improved sentiment but structural NIM headwinds persist.
optimistic_case.portfolio_action: In this scenario, ASML and NVDA become immediate WATCH-to-entry candidates. XLE entry window closes further — avoid adding at extended levels even on a rally.

pessimistic_case.description: Hormuz closure extends beyond 4 months; Iran successfully mines or blockades the strait; WTI spikes back toward $120-135; global shipping costs surge; CPI re-accelerates, forcing Fed to hold rates through year-end 2026.
pessimistic_case.market_impact: SPY breaks 6,500 support and tests 6,200-6,300; VIX spikes above 30; tech selloff deepens (AMZN, GOOG test lower supports); Singapore airlines (C6L) face severe revenue hit; XLE initially spikes but then reverses as demand destruction accelerates.
pessimistic_case.portfolio_action: Pause all new entries across the watchlist. If SPY breaks $660, the only watchlist names that hold value are PLTR (defense spending surge) and potentially INTC/MU on the US domestic supply chain thesis. Avoid energy at elevated levels — XLE likely reverses hard if demand destruction accelerates.

wildcard.description: US Supreme Court or Congressional challenge to the Section 122 tariff authority (building on the IEEPA ruling precedent cited in macro context) removes the legal basis for 100% China tariffs within 60-90 days, triggering a sharp reversal in the US-domestic-semis rotation trade.
wildcard.market_impact: INTC and MU lose their US supply chain premium; ASML China export restriction risk partially re-prices; AMD potentially re-rates upward as China sales restrictions ease; broader tech rally but highly uneven across sub-sectors.
wildcard.portfolio_action: Singapore names and extended positions like LITE and COHR face sharpest forced selling in the initial reaction. NVDA would be the clearest beneficiary. Do not pre-position for this scenario — monitor legal developments and react only on confirmed rulings.

events_this_week.event: CPI Report (February) — released today; critical inflection point for Fed rate-cut expectations and tech multiple sustainability
WTI crude stabilising near $93 after -30% decline from $120+ highs — demand destruction vs. geopolitical supply risk in balance
SPY below 100-day moving average — unresolved technical breakdown; 6,500 support level remains the downside target
VIX at 24.23 — elevated, reflecting persistent geopolitical tail risk from Hormuz conflict entering second week
COHR profit-taking accelerating (-5.47% today, -8.53% five-day) — photonics cluster entering post-S&P inclusion consolidation phase
PPI Report (February) — producer price inflation; secondary read on pipeline price pressures following today's CPI
FOMC Rate Decision — Fed expected to hold; statement language on inflation trajectory and Hormuz impact will drive rate-sensitive tech names
AMD Ryzen AI 400 Series desktop launch — per AMD MWC 2026 press release; OEM availability from Dell, HP, Lenovo; potential re-rating catalyst if AI PC demand materialises
INTC High-NA EUV (Intel 14A node) ramp with ASML Twinscan EXE:5200B — per ASML Q4 2025 earnings call and Intel investor day; technology leadership inflection for both names
AMD MI450 GPU shipments (reportedly for Meta supply deal — unverified, needs SEC filing) — would validate AMD as hyperscale-viable NVDA alternative
NVDA Blackwell Ultra (B300) volume production ramp — per NVDA Q4 FY2026 earnings call; Vera Rubin samples already shipping to customers

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously defensive with selective semi and photonics watches
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: The Iran-Hormuz conflict entering its second week keeps geopolitical tail risk elevated (VIX 24.23), while the unresolved S&P 500 technical breakdown below the 100-day moving average means broad index exposure is unattractive — the only near-term entry candidates are ASML (2.1% above its 50-day average) and C6L (0.76% above its 50-day average), both of which require patience given low volume signals.

watch_for_entry.note: Best entry geometry in the semis cluster at 2.1% above 50-day average. Half position — China export control overhang persists, wait for volume confirmation.
Only near-entry candidate in Singapore cluster; sitting on 50-day average support at SGD 6.62. Half position if it pulls back — Gulf airspace disruption is an active headwind.
Upgrades to Watch on a pullback to $177-180 support. Currently at HOLD — Blackwell Ultra ramp thesis intact but no entry dislocation exists at current price.
Two sessions away from the 30-session trend exception threshold. Becomes actionable at SGD 4.80-4.90 on any pullback or after 30 confirmed sessions above the 50-day average.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Target entry SGD 4.80-4.90, stop below SGD 4.74

watch_for_entry.note: Entry blocked at current levels (5%+ above 50-day average, RSI at boundary). Becomes actionable on a pullback to $44-46 near the 50-day average.
Entry blocked at 9.3% extension. Wait for pullback to $363-382 where 50-day average and prior support converge. Forward price-to-earnings of 8.9x attractive if memory cycle holds.
Profit-taking underway (-8.5% five-day). Watch for entry at $210-225 near the 50-day average as consolidation continues. Unverified NVDA investment needs 8-K confirmation.
RSI at 67.3 keeps entry blocked. Actionable if XLE pulls back to $54-56 with RSI cooling below 60 — right thesis, wrong entry point today.
Extreme 35% extension — no entry. Wait for pullback to at least $620-640 before reconsidering. S&P inclusion and record revenue are confirmed; NVDA investment unverified.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Target entry $620-640 on meaningful correction, stop below $528

caution_trim.note: Avoid — declining 50-day average, net interest margin compression ongoing, rate pause removes the core bank margin catalyst. Next support at SGD 51.72 (200-day average).
Avoid — just slipped below 50-day average, removing the entry zone that justified the prior WATCH signal. Revisit if it reclaims SGD 20.85 with volume.
caution_trim.entry_level: Not attractive until reclaim of SGD 20.85

caution_trim.note: Avoid — 6.54% one-month decline, below declining 50-day average. Cheapest Singapore bank on forward earnings (9.9x) but trend must stabilise first.
Avoid — declining 50-day average, 78.8x trailing earnings, unverified Meta deal. Re-enter watchlist only if AMD reclaims $216 with volume.
Avoid — declining 50-day and 200-day averages simultaneously. $211 support being tested; a break opens a move to $197.
caution_trim.entry_level: Not attractive until reclaim of $224 (50-day average)

caution_trim.note: Avoid — declining 50-day average in a rising-rate environment compresses the growth premium. $308 is the near-term line; below it, $237 support comes into view.
caution_trim.entry_level: Not attractive until reclaim of $319 (50-day average)

caution_trim.note: Avoid broad index exposure — technical breakdown unresolved, VIX elevated at 24.23. Wait for SPY to reclaim 100-day moving average with multi-session follow-through.
caution_trim.entry_level: Not attractive until SPY reclaims 100-day moving average

hold_no_action.note: No action — price hugging 50-day average at $185.70, RSI neutral at 51. Blackwell Ultra ramp thesis intact. Upgrades to Watch on pullback to $177-180.
hold_no_action.entry_level: No entry at current level; watch $177-180

hold_no_action.note: No action — defense AI thesis strong but 82x forward earnings prices in most of the upside. IDIQ framework ceiling is not firm revenue. No entry until pullback to $140-145 or firm contract awards appear in quarterly revenue.
hold_no_action.entry_level: No entry above $145; monitor for IDIQ-to-firm conversion

new_stocks_to_watch.note: INTC foundry ambitions and ASML High-NA EUV ramp both depend on TSMC setting the leading-edge production pace. First-order connection to semis cluster.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_level: Add to watchlist if TSM pulls back 8-10% from current levels on a broad semi correction

new_stocks_to_watch.note: Broadcom integrates LITE and COHR optical components into AI datacenter networking chips — photonics cluster acceleration flows directly into Broadcom's custom AI revenue.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_level: Interesting if Broadcom pulls back 8-12% from current levels; revisit after next hyperscaler capital expenditure update for optical demand confirmation

new_stocks_to_watch.note: Most direct single-stock expression of the Hormuz oil supply thesis captured by XLE — highest earnings sensitivity to WTI recovery above $100.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_level: Monitor for entry if WTI stabilises above $95 for three or more consecutive sessions and XOM pulls back 5% from current levels
